projects.title: IR Universal Remote
projects.tagline: Learn any button from your TV remote, then blast it back from your wrist.
projects.howItWorks: StickS3 has both an IR receiver and an IR transmitter built in. Point any remote at it to capture the raw IR code, save it to flash under a named slot, and send it back on a button press. A short list on the LCD lets you scroll through stored buttons.
extensions: Share codes between two StickS3s over ESP-NOW
Macro button that fires a sequence (mute + switch input)

projects.title: Pocket Step Counter + Tilt Level
projects.tagline: Step counter by day, digital bubble level by night — entirely on the IMU.
projects.howItWorks: The 6-axis IMU gives you accelerometer + gyro. Low-pass the vertical axis to count steps, and read tilt angle in a second 'Level' mode. Long-press the side button to switch modes, show a clean big-digit screen for each.
extensions: Log daily step totals to flash
Vibrate the speaker as haptic feedback on each step

projects.title: Voice-to-Morse Trainer
projects.tagline: Say a word; StickS3 chirps it back in morse code through the speaker.
projects.howItWorks: Sample the built-in MEMS mic for short word input, do a tiny keyword-spotting or just detect speech-vs-silence to estimate letter count, then play morse-coded beeps. The LCD draws dots and dashes in real time as a visual aid.
extensions: Flash the LCD for deaf learners instead of beeping
Morse 'decoder' mode that listens to beeps and shows letters
Beam morse over IR between two StickS3s

projects.title: Pocket Notes Terminal
projects.tagline: A tiny keyboard-first notepad that saves to flash — no cloud, no distractions.
projects.howItWorks: Use the 56-key keyboard to type into a scrolling text buffer. Ctrl+S writes the buffer to a named slot in flash; arrow keys scroll through notes. Great first non-trivial embedded app.
learningGoals: text rendering on a tiny LCD
extensions: Export notes over USB serial to your laptop

projects.title: Chiptune Keyboard
projects.tagline: Turn the Cardputer into a pocket synth — one row per octave, keys play square waves.
projects.howItWorks: Map the QWERTY row to one octave, number row to the next. Each key press tells the onboard DAC to emit a square-wave at the matching frequency. Draw a piano-roll on the LCD so you can see the chord you're holding.
extensions: Record and loop a short riff
Second keyboard for duet mode over ESP-NOW

projects.title: Always-On Wi-Fi Clock
projects.tagline: NTP-synced big-digit clock with weather from a public API — a perfect first network build.
projects.howItWorks: Connect to Wi-Fi, sync time via NTP, draw a full-screen clock on the LCD. Optionally fetch a free weather API once per hour and show the current temperature underneath. Dim the backlight at night.
extensions: Pomodoro mode (25 m work / 5 m break)
Calendar pull from a Google Calendar feed

projects.title: Desk Climate Display
projects.tagline: A tiny always-on temperature, humidity, and air-pressure readout for your desk.
projects.howItWorks: The ENV III Unit talks to the device over I2C. Your code reads the SHT30 for temperature and humidity and the QMP6988 for pressure, then redraws a simple dashboard on the LCD once per second.
extensions: Push readings to MQTT over Wi-Fi
Log to flash and graph a 24 h trend
Beep when the room gets stuffy

projects.title: Motion-Activated Night Light
projects.tagline: Walk past it, the RGB strip fades on; stay still, it fades back off.
projects.howItWorks: The PIR sensor pulls a GPIO high when it detects motion. A non-blocking timer counts down after each trigger and drives a warm-white gradient on the SK6812 strip via the NeoPixel protocol.
extensions: Log motion events to the serial console

projects.title: Distance Radar Game
projects.tagline: An ultrasonic 'proximity bar' that turns red as things get too close.
projects.howItWorks: The I2C ultrasonic unit returns distance in mm. You map the reading onto the number of lit LEDs, and shift the hue from green to red as the object closes in.
extensions: Add a score that ticks up when something stays in the 'danger zone'
Mount on a door frame as a rear-approach warning
Turn it into a 'Marco Polo' toy with two devices

projects.title: Joystick Pong
projects.tagline: Classic Pong on the device LCD, one-player versus CPU.
projects.howItWorks: The I2C Joystick 2 returns X/Y analog positions and a button state. Your game loop reads them, updates the paddle and ball physics, and renders the frame to the LCD.

projects.title: USB Volume Knob for Your PC
projects.tagline: Twist the Angle knob to raise or lower your computer volume.
projects.howItWorks: The Angle Unit exposes a 10K pot as an analog voltage. Your firmware samples it, and either emits BLE HID media keys or sends volume commands over serial to a tiny companion script on the PC.
extensions: Add an on-screen level bar on the LCD

projects.title: Classroom Light Level Logger
projects.tagline: A science-fair-ready light meter that graphs a full day on its screen.
projects.howItWorks: The Light Unit outputs an analog voltage proportional to brightness. Sample it every 30 s, push readings into a ring buffer, and draw a scrolling line chart on the LCD. The Mini Proto Unit gives you a place to solder a calibration trimmer if you want matched readings.
extensions: Dump CSV over serial for a classroom write-up
Automatically pull a blind with a servo

projects.title: Smart NFC Locker
projects.tagline: Tap a card, the latch swings open. Great first home-security build.
projects.howItWorks: The ST25R3916 NFC Unit reports any tag's UID over I2C. If the UID is in your allow-list, the firmware pulses the SG90 servo for 5 s to swing a cardboard or 3D-printed latch open.
extensions: Log allowed / denied taps with timestamps
Fire the relay to power a mag-lock instead of a servo
Flash the RGB strip green for pass, red for fail

projects.title: Self-Regulating Relay Fan
projects.tagline: Room too hot? A fan on the relay kicks in automatically.
projects.howItWorks: Read temperature from the ENV III. When it crosses a threshold, close one channel of the SPST relay to power a mains-rated fan. A 2 °C hysteresis band stops the relay chattering near the setpoint.
extensions: Add a schedule so the fan won't run at night
Second relay channel for a humidifier
Web dashboard to change the setpoint from a phone

projects.title: PIR Intruder Alarm
projects.tagline: A lightshow plus optional Wi-Fi push when the sensor sees movement.
projects.howItWorks: PIR trip arms a cool-down timer, flashes the RGB strip red, and, if Wi-Fi is configured, posts a webhook to ntfy.sh or your own endpoint. A long button press arms/disarms the system.
extensions: Pair with an NFC tag as a silent disarm fob
Photograph the scene with a companion device
Log to a simple timeline on the LCD

projects.title: Automatic Plant Waterer
projects.tagline: Keeps a thirsty plant alive while you're on vacation.
projects.howItWorks: Humidity from the ENV III stands in for soil dryness (paired with a DIY resistive probe on the Mini Proto if you want to get fancy). When the reading drops below threshold, the servo tilts a reservoir, or the relay kicks a small pump on.
extensions: Add a weekly water-log on the LCD
Push a 'Plant saved!' notification each watering
Go solar + LiPo for outdoor pots

projects.title: Visitor Counter
projects.tagline: Count people walking by a doorway and show a live tally.
projects.howItWorks: The ultrasonic unit watches for any return below the doorway-width threshold. A debounced counter increments on each new entry, and the strip animates a quick green sweep for feedback.
extensions: Split entries vs. exits with two sensors
Mount on a classroom door to track attendance trends

projects.title: Mood Lamp Controller
projects.tagline: Twist to pick a colour, push to save it as a preset.
projects.howItWorks: The Angle potentiometer maps onto the hue wheel. The LCD shows a colour-matched preview. Long-pressing the device button stores the current colour into one of three flash slots, recalled by double-press.
extensions: Sunset simulator mode tied to real time

projects.title: 6-Sensor Home Dashboard
projects.tagline: The I/O Hub turns one I2C port into a small sensor network.
projects.howItWorks: The PbHub-based I/O Hub fans one HY2.0-4P out to six PortB channels. Each channel hosts one Unit; the firmware rotates through an on-LCD dashboard with a tile per sensor.
learningGoals: UI layout on a small screen
extensions: Publish all readings to Home Assistant over MQTT
Alert tile when any sensor goes out of range

projects.title: Reaction-Time Arcade
projects.tagline: Joystick + LED light show = a party-ready reflex game.
projects.howItWorks: A random LED lights up; the player has to flick the joystick in the matching direction before it goes out. Speed ramps with each correct hit. Miss three and the round ends.
extensions: Sound effects via the Cardputer speaker

projects.title: LoRa Walkie-Talkie (Cardputer)
projects.tagline: Type short messages to a friend's Cardputer, no Wi-Fi needed.
projects.howItWorks: The SX1262 in the Cap LoRa sends short packets on 868/915 MHz. The Cardputer keyboard becomes your input; received messages scroll up the LCD as a chat log.
extensions: Add a 6-digit shared secret so only paired devices see each other
Compress messages with a dictionary codec

projects.title: Off-Grid GPS Trail Logger
projects.tagline: Hike, record a GPS track, share snippets over LoRa.
projects.howItWorks: The Cap LoRa bundles an ATGM336H GNSS receiver alongside the radio. Parse NMEA strings, stamp them with elapsed time, and periodically beacon your position over LoRa so a base-station partner can follow along.
extensions: Export GPX for Strava / Komoot on return
On-screen mini-map drawn from the track
Distress button that pings base station at max power

projects.title: Field Weather Station with LoRa Uplink
projects.tagline: Deploy in the backyard, read it from inside the house.
projects.howItWorks: Outside: ENV III + optional Light Unit feeding an hourly LoRa packet. Inside: a second Cardputer (or any LoRa-capable device) receives, displays, and logs the readings. Great for comparing a garden microclimate to indoor air.
extensions: Add a rain-puck switch on the Mini Proto
Push to a cloud dashboard through a gateway

projects.title: Classroom Attendance Kiosk
projects.tagline: Students tap a lanyard card; the door chime and a green LED confirm.
projects.howItWorks: The I/O Hub fans out to the NFC reader, the RGB strip, and the relay (chime). When a known UID taps, attendance is marked, the strip flashes green, and the relay clicks a short chime. Unknown UIDs get a red flash and no chime.
extensions: Sync attendance to a Google Sheet via Wi-Fi
Support visitor badges with expiry timestamps
Touchscreen admin menu to enrol new cards

projects.title: Joystick-Driven Puppet Arm
projects.tagline: Two servos form an X/Y pan-tilt controlled by the joystick.
projects.howItWorks: Two SG90 servos mount on a printed or laser-cut frame. The I/O Hub hosts both servos plus the joystick; firmware maps joystick X/Y to servo angles with rate limiting for smooth motion.
extensions: Record and replay a motion sequence
Add a third servo as a gripper and control it with the Z-axis button
Trigger automatic 'look-at' behaviour based on the PIR sensor

projects.title: Home Zone Controller
projects.tagline: One device watches a room; turns things on, warns on anomalies.
projects.howItWorks: The Mini Hub expands one port into three, hosting the PIR, ENV III, and relay at once. Motion + heat above threshold fires the relay (e.g., an AC); no motion for 10 minutes turns it back off. Anomalies (too hot, too humid) flash the RGB strip and raise a message on the LCD.
extensions: Integrate with Home Assistant over MQTT
Graph a week of activity on-device
Use NFC card to set 'Home' / 'Away' modes

projects.title: Pet Traffic Logger
projects.tagline: Count how many times the cat walks through the hallway — surprisingly revealing.
projects.howItWorks: A PIR trip bumps a daily counter and timestamps the event into a flash log. The LCD shows today, yesterday, and a tiny bar chart. Add ENV III to correlate activity with room temperature.
extensions: Push a daily summary to your phone
Distinguish cat vs. human using timing heuristics

projects.title: Hallway Auto-Dim Strip
projects.tagline: Step into the hall and the strip fades up; the brightness knob sets your cap.
projects.howItWorks: PIR motion triggers a smooth fade-in to the user-set brightness (from the Angle knob). After 90 s of no motion, the strip fades back to zero. A gentle warm-white avoids blinding anyone at 3 a.m.
extensions: Night mode: dim red tone after 10 p.m.

projects.title: Desk Presence "Do Not Disturb"
projects.tagline: You sit down → your door lights glow red. You leave → green for 'come in'.
projects.howItWorks: PIR pointed at your chair. When motion is detected for more than 20 s, the strip turns red (busy). When no motion for 3 minutes, it cross-fades to green.
extensions: Pomodoro-aware: enforce red during focus blocks
Red = do not disturb, yellow = coding, blue = meeting

projects.title: Wildlife Camera Trigger
projects.tagline: Motion snaps a DSLR for you — perfect for backyard fox or feeder shots.
projects.howItWorks: The PIR detects movement; the relay pulses a DSLR shutter release (2.5 mm plug) for 200 ms, with a configurable cool-down so you don't get 400 bird photos.
extensions: Add an IR-cut mode for night shots
Log every trigger with a timestamp

projects.title: Porch Package Alert
projects.tagline: A PIR on the porch pings your phone the moment a delivery lands.
projects.howItWorks: PIR points at your doorstep. On trigger, the device POSTs to a free ntfy.sh topic; your phone buzzes in seconds. A 2-minute cool-down avoids spam from the courier walking back to their van.
extensions: Photograph the scene with a companion camera
Vacation mode that sends all events
Quiet hours between 11 p.m. and 6 a.m.

projects.title: Thunderstorm Early-Warning
projects.tagline: A fast pressure drop tells you 20–40 minutes before the storm arrives.
projects.howItWorks: Read the QMP6988 pressure sensor every minute. When the 10-minute moving average drops more than ~2 hPa, flash a warning on the LCD and beep. Surprisingly accurate for low-pressure fronts.
extensions: Log storms and correlate with weather.gov data
Add a lightning-bolt animation on trigger

projects.title: Bread-Proofing Monitor
projects.tagline: Glows green when your dough is in the sourdough-perfect 24–27 °C / 70%+ RH window.
projects.howItWorks: ENV III lives inside (or next to) a proofing box. A tiny on-LCD graph plots temp and humidity over the last 4 hours. RGB strip is green when both are in the target band, amber when one drifts, red when both do.
extensions: Share the session's graph as a PNG

projects.title: Greenhouse Min/Max Logger
projects.tagline: Daily high, low, and sunlight totals for your tomatoes — science-fair ready.
projects.howItWorks: Sample temperature, humidity, and light every 5 minutes. Track daily min/max for each, and accumulate 'light-hours' (time where lux crosses a threshold). Reset at midnight, keep the last 7 days on the LCD.
extensions: Predict harvest date from growing degree days

projects.title: Baby-Room Comfort Dashboard
projects.tagline: At a glance: blue = too cold, red = too hot, green = just right.
projects.howItWorks: ENV III reads temperature + humidity. The RGB strip mounts above the crib, showing colour-coded comfort: blue (<18 °C), green (18–22 °C), amber (22–25 °C), red (>25 °C or humidity out of band).
extensions: Gentle sunrise pulse at baby's wake-up time

projects.title: Cat Toy Swinger
projects.tagline: A servo flicks a feather-on-a-string every few minutes — your cat will approve.
projects.howItWorks: SG90 sweeps back and forth on a randomised schedule (say, every 3–8 minutes) with a random arc so the cat can't predict it. Off at night. You can print a tiny arm or just hot-glue a chopstick.
extensions: Vary the pattern by time of day
Pair with PIR so it only plays when the cat is nearby

projects.title: Joystick Camera Pan-Tilt
projects.tagline: Two servos and a joystick turn a phone into a remote-control tripod head.
projects.howItWorks: The joystick's X/Y analog values map to servo angles with rate limiting for smooth motion. A 3D-printed or laser-cut pan/tilt bracket holds two SG90s and a phone clamp.
extensions: Save and replay a camera move
Auto-track: lock on to the loudest mic direction

projects.title: Desk Status Flipper
projects.tagline: A tiny servo flips a 2-sided sign: 'In a meeting' ↔ 'Come on in'.
projects.howItWorks: A printed sign sits on the servo arm. Pressing the device button rotates 0°→180°, flipping the sign. The device screen mirrors the current state so you can check from your desk without looking up.
extensions: Calendar-aware: auto-flip when a meeting starts
Four-sided sign: working / lunch / meeting / away
Tap NFC card to change state

projects.title: Analog Mood Gauge
projects.tagline: A servo-driven needle points to current room temp on a printed dial — like a barometer.
projects.howItWorks: ENV III reads temperature; you map 15–30 °C onto 0–180° of servo rotation. Print a dial face with tick marks. Smooth filtering so the needle drifts rather than jitters.
extensions: Show your unread email count as 'mood'

projects.title: NFC-Tap Pet Treat Dispenser
projects.tagline: Kid taps the card, dog gets a treat — one well-trained family.
projects.howItWorks: A servo drives a gate on the bottom of a treat hopper. An allowed NFC card (kid's or parent's) rotates the gate open for 0.5 s, dropping a treat. A cool-down prevents spam.
extensions: Log treats per day per card
Speaker plays a jingle on each dispense

projects.title: Aquarium Pump Scheduler
projects.tagline: Run the pump 15 min on, 45 min off — reefs love it, electric bill smiles.
projects.howItWorks: The relay switches a low-voltage aquarium pump on/off to a configurable duty cycle. A small on-LCD counter shows hours of runtime today.
extensions: Failsafe that alerts if the relay stops clicking

projects.title: Smart Grow-Light Timer
projects.tagline: Grow lights kick in only when the room dims — perfect for cloudy winter days.
projects.howItWorks: Light Unit watches ambient brightness. When sustained below a threshold between 06:00 and 20:00, the relay powers your grow lights. Logs total 'bonus' hours per day.
extensions: Per-plant schedules from a config menu
MQTT dashboard for the whole plant shelf
Self-adjusting threshold based on weekly averages

projects.title: NFC Garage Door Opener
projects.tagline: Forget the remote — tap your phone's NFC chip to open the door.
projects.howItWorks: The NFC Unit recognises authorised UIDs. On a match, the relay pulses the dry-contact input on your garage opener for 250 ms. A rolling audit log shows the last 20 taps.
extensions: Guest card that expires after 24 h

projects.title: Humidifier Auto-On
projects.tagline: Whenever the room falls below 40% RH, the relay flicks the humidifier on.
projects.howItWorks: ENV III watches humidity. When below the setpoint for more than 2 minutes, the relay switches the humidifier on. Turns off at setpoint + 5% hysteresis band. Emergency cut-off if RH exceeds 70%.
extensions: Learn your house's natural RH drift

projects.title: NFC Tap-to-Play Jukebox
projects.tagline: Each card triggers a song or playlist — retro vibe with zero apps.
projects.howItWorks: A tapped UID maps to a command sent over Wi-Fi to your media server (Home Assistant / Jellyfin / Sonos HTTP API). The Cardputer LCD shows the now-playing card art.
extensions: Kids' cards: only play approved playlists
Tap two cards to queue them
Record the session's tap log as a party journal

projects.title: Kid's NFC Chore Chart
projects.tagline: Tap a card when a chore is done — the LED strip fills as the week goes on.
projects.howItWorks: Each chore has its own NFC card. Tapping adds one LED to the strip in that chore's colour. At week's end, a celebratory rainbow sweep plays and the strip resets.
extensions: Photo-proof pairing: require a phone photo before tap counts

projects.title: Pomodoro NFC Starter
projects.tagline: Tap a card on your desk to start a 25-minute focus block — no app needed.
projects.howItWorks: Different NFC cards kick off different work blocks (25 / 50 / 90 min). The LCD shows the countdown in big digits; at zero, a short chime plays. Tap again to pause.
extensions: Enforce silent mode during the block
Share sessions with a study partner over Wi-Fi

projects.title: Pocket Inventory Scanner
projects.tagline: Tap tagged items, type quantity on the keyboard, export a CSV — stockroom in a pocket.
projects.howItWorks: Each item wears a small NFC tag with a known UID-to-name mapping. Tap → name shows on LCD → keyboard accepts a qty → press Enter to commit. The Cardputer stores rows; USB serial dumps them as CSV.
learningGoals: data entry UX on a tiny device
extensions: Enrol new tags on the fly
Per-tag photos from a phone app
Wi-Fi sync to a Google Sheet

projects.title: Daily Check-In Tag
projects.tagline: Mum taps her wellness card every morning; if not, the family gets a gentle ping.
projects.howItWorks: An NFC card on the kettle / pill box registers a daily tap. If 10 a.m. passes without one, the device POSTs a check-in prompt to the family channel. ENV III reads room temperature to add 'all fine, 21 °C' to the ping.
extensions: Escalate to phone call if multiple misses

projects.title: Garage Parking Assist
projects.tagline: Drive in — green, yellow, red. Park like a valet, every time.
projects.howItWorks: The ultrasonic sits at the front of the garage, aimed at the car. Distance maps to strip colour: green (safe), yellow (slow), red (stop). A flashing red = too close.

projects.title: Water Tank Level Monitor
projects.tagline: A single ultrasonic on the lid reports the percent-full of your rain barrel or well.
projects.howItWorks: Mount the ultrasonic pointing down into the tank. Calibrate empty and full distances, then show a vertical % bar on the LCD and a number. A waterproof enclosure is essential.
extensions: Pair with a relay to auto-refill

projects.title: Desk Posture Reminder
projects.tagline: When you lean too close to the screen, a gentle beep nudges you back.
projects.howItWorks: Ultrasonic on the monitor points at your face. If you're inside 40 cm for more than 30 s, a soft chirp + on-screen nudge prompts a small lean-back. 10-minute mute button to silence it during close-up work.
extensions: Auto-mute when you're on a call
Gamify: streaks for good posture days

projects.title: Mailbox Package Alert
projects.tagline: Packed delivery sensed → phone buzz. Works on any mailbox / porch / side door.
projects.howItWorks: Ultrasonic mounted inside the mailbox reads 'empty distance'. A measurable decrease + a timeout filter = a package landed. POSTs a ntfy notification to your phone.

projects.title: Ultrasonic Pocket Theremin
projects.tagline: Wave your hand — pitch bends. It's the world's tiniest physics demo.
projects.howItWorks: Ultrasonic distance maps to audio frequency via the onboard speaker. Log-mapping keeps the pitch musical rather than linear. A button toggles between continuous and snapped-to-notes modes.

projects.title: Obstacle-Avoiding Rover
projects.tagline: A simple two-servo rover that drives itself around the room without bumping.
projects.howItWorks: Two continuous-rotation servos (or a standard SG90 pair on a small chassis) drive the rover. The ultrasonic scans ahead; detecting an obstacle closer than 25 cm triggers a turn-until-clear routine.
extensions: Add a second ultrasonic for side sensing

projects.title: LoRa Channel Tuner
projects.tagline: Twist the knob to hop LoRa frequencies — great for field-testing your radio.
projects.howItWorks: The Angle Unit's value maps to a LoRa channel index. As you turn, the SX1262 retunes; the LCD shows current MHz and RSSI of the last packet heard. Pair two devices to test link quality.
extensions: Auto-scan mode that finds quietest channel

projects.title: Music Scrub Knob
projects.tagline: Twist to scrub through the current song on your phone or laptop.
projects.howItWorks: Angle maps to a relative playhead position. Changes emit BLE HID media commands (skip back / skip forward) or directly control the Spotify Web API over Wi-Fi. Large delta = bigger jump.

projects.title: Direct Servo Knob
projects.tagline: Twist the Angle knob, the servo follows 1:1. Perfect intro to analog→PWM.
projects.howItWorks: Read the Angle analog value, map it to 0–180°, and drive the SG90 PWM. The LCD shows both numbers so you see the relationship live.
extensions: Record a knob-drawn motion and loop it

projects.title: Dimmer + Relay Smart Lamp
projects.tagline: Knob dims the RGB strip; device button kicks a wall-lamp relay on or off.
projects.howItWorks: The Angle value drives the RGB strip brightness via PWM. A double-click on the device button toggles the AC lamp via the relay. Ambient mood + main light in one node.

projects.title: Tempo Metronome Knob
projects.tagline: Twist to dial BPM from 40 to 220 — a drummer's best desk companion.
projects.howItWorks: Angle maps to BPM. The onboard speaker plays a click with every beat, and the LCD flashes a colour accent on the downbeat. Long-press: double-time mode.
extensions: Add a second 'beats per bar' knob via I/O Hub

projects.title: Classroom Mood Knob
projects.tagline: Every student turns a knob from 0 (rough day) to 10 — teacher sees a class heat-map.
projects.howItWorks: A single Angle-equipped device is passed around. Each student sets their value and taps confirm. Values go to the LCD as anonymous bars; a final tally shows class-average mood.
extensions: Teacher dashboard on a second device
Triggers a 'need a break?' suggestion below a threshold

projects.title: Pocket Pixel-Art Editor
projects.tagline: Doodle 32×32 pixel art on the LCD with the joystick — export to PNG over USB.
projects.howItWorks: The joystick moves a cursor; the press-button toggles a pixel. Z-axis (joystick click) cycles palette colours. Keyboard shortcuts save / load sprites from flash; USB serial exports PNG bytes.

projects.title: Pocket Presentation Clicker
projects.tagline: Joystick left/right = previous/next slide over BLE — no third-party dongle.
projects.howItWorks: The joystick's X direction sends BLE HID PageUp / PageDown. Push-down = start/stop the onscreen timer. The LCD shows the elapsed time of your talk so you don't run long.
extensions: Buzz at 80% of the talk length
Pair with a wearable laser pointer

projects.title: LCD Maze Runner
projects.tagline: Navigate random mazes on the tiny screen — a calming 10-minute lunch game.
projects.howItWorks: A random maze is generated using recursive backtracking. The joystick steers a dot. RGB strip reflects remaining time or hint count. Reaching the goal advances to a bigger maze.

projects.title: Joystick RGB Remote
projects.tagline: One stick controls an entire RGB strip's colour, speed, and animation.
projects.howItWorks: Joystick X = hue, Y = brightness, Z (press) cycles effects (solid / chase / pulse). The LCD shows a colour-wheel cursor. Save up to 4 favourite scenes to buttons.
extensions: Two strips, one for each zone

projects.title: Digital Etch-a-Sketch
projects.tagline: Draw by tilting the joystick; shake the Cardputer to erase. Pure nostalgia.
projects.howItWorks: Joystick direction extends a single line on the LCD. Unlike traditional etch-a-sketch, you can change colour with the Z-button. Accelerometer-like shake not available — instead, pressing Delete clears the screen.

projects.title: Auto-Dim Bedroom Lights
projects.tagline: Sun goes down → lamp warms and dims automatically. No hub, no subscription.
projects.howItWorks: Light Unit samples brightness. Below a threshold (sustained), the relay kicks on a lamp on a dimmer module. Time-of-day check avoids false triggers during cloudy afternoons.
extensions: Pair with the Dimmer + Relay Lamp project

projects.title: Reading-Light Coach
projects.tagline: Kid reading in bad light? A soft amber glow reminds them to turn on the lamp.
projects.howItWorks: Light Unit sits on the reader's desk. When lux stays below a safe-reading threshold for 90 s, the RGB strip pulses gently. Tap the device button to snooze for 30 min.
extensions: Auto-turn a lamp on via relay

projects.title: Plant Photon-Hours Counter
projects.tagline: Track how many sun-hours your monstera got today — plant-parenting by the numbers.
projects.howItWorks: Sample lux every 30 s. Add 0.5 minutes to the counter when the reading is above 'sun threshold'. Display today's total and a 7-day rolling chart on the LCD. Reset at midnight.
extensions: Compare two plants side by side

projects.title: Laser Trip-Wire Alarm
projects.tagline: Beam a laser pointer at the Light Unit; break the beam and LEDs flash red.
projects.howItWorks: A cheap laser pointer hits the Light Unit across a doorway. A sudden drop below the calibrated floor triggers the RGB strip to flash red and play a buzz. Reset on a re-acquired beam.
extensions: Mesh with PIR for secondary validation

projects.title: Sun-Tracking Heliostat
projects.tagline: A servo slowly aims a mirror at the sun all day — free daylight into a shaded room.
projects.howItWorks: Two light sensors flank a mirror. The servo rotates toward the brighter side in tiny increments, keeping the mirror aimed at the sun via a simple 'phototropic' feedback loop.
extensions: Mount a tiny solar panel instead of a mirror

projects.title: 6-Zone Smart Irrigation
projects.tagline: Water each garden bed only when it needs it — and not on rainy days.
projects.howItWorks: The I/O Hub fans out to up to six relays (each driving one solenoid valve) and to ENV III + Light. A per-zone schedule runs, but is suppressed if humidity is too high or a drop in pressure suggests incoming rain.

projects.title: Multi-Servo Animatronic
projects.tagline: A puppet that waves and lights up when someone walks past — perfect Halloween flex.
projects.howItWorks: PIR triggers a scripted sequence: two SG90 servos animate eyes + arm; the RGB strip pulses like a breath. The I/O Hub hosts them all on one I²C lane. Cool-down of 20 s before the next trigger.
extensions: Add a speaker for spooky SFX

projects.title: DIY MIDI Knob Box
projects.tagline: Four knobs over the I/O Hub become USB MIDI CC controls for any DAW.
projects.howItWorks: Four Angle Units plug into the I/O Hub. Each reading maps to a configurable MIDI CC number. USB MIDI HID sends the values to your DAW in real time. The LCD shows live values.
extensions: Save a kit per DAW preset

projects.title: Thermal Multi-Fan Controller
projects.tagline: Three 12 V fans ramp on staged thresholds — AV cabinet stays cool, whisper-quiet.
projects.howItWorks: ENV III reads ambient temp. As thresholds are crossed (28 °C / 32 °C / 36 °C), the I/O Hub triggers relay channels that power successive fans. Hysteresis prevents rapid cycling.

projects.title: Interactive Art Wall
projects.tagline: Visitors approach → LEDs bloom toward them; motion sprinkles sparkles across the wall.
projects.howItWorks: PIR detects nearby motion; ultrasonic gives distance. A ripple animation centred on the closest moving shape plays on the RGB strip, tapering intensity with distance. All wired through the I/O Hub.
extensions: Leave a trail behind the viewer

projects.title: DIY Soil Moisture Probe
projects.tagline: Two nails, a resistor, and the Mini Proto become a surprisingly good soil probe.
projects.howItWorks: Solder two stainless-steel nails with a series resistor on the Mini Proto. Read the voltage divider as an analog value. Calibrate 'dry' and 'soaked' to map to percentage.

projects.title: DIY Reed-Switch Door Sensor
projects.tagline: A magnet + a reed switch + Mini Proto = a classic door/window open-close detector.
projects.howItWorks: A reed switch soldered on the Mini Proto closes when a magnet (on the moving part of the door) is near. The device reads the GPIO and logs open/close events with timestamps.
extensions: Multi-door kit (one Mini Proto per door)
Alert when door left open >5 min

projects.title: DIY Button Keypad
projects.tagline: Build your own 3×3 keypad for custom macros, shortcuts, or a party buzzer.
projects.howItWorks: Nine tactile switches wired in a 3×3 matrix on the Mini Proto. The firmware scans rows/columns, debounces, and maps to keyboard macros (over BLE HID) or in-device actions.
extensions: RGB halo via a short RGB strip around the edges

projects.title: DIY Touch Sensor
projects.tagline: Tape a foil pad to the Mini Proto and you've got a capacitive touch button — touch a plant, trigger a beep.
projects.howItWorks: Wire a high-value resistor and a conductive pad on the Mini Proto. The firmware measures RC charge-discharge time through the ESP32-S3 touch peripheral (or polled GPIO) to detect finger presence.

projects.title: DIY Custom Button Box
projects.tagline: A labelled, back-lit button panel for your streaming or smart-home setup.
projects.howItWorks: Four buttons on the Mini Proto trigger macros (scene switch, mute, donation sound, end show). An RGB strip behind the panel lights each zone to indicate active scene / recording state.
extensions: Hot-swap labels via a printable sticker sheet

projects.title: DIY Tilt-Ball Sensor
projects.tagline: A steel ball in a tube becomes a wake-the-device tilt switch.
projects.howItWorks: Solder two contacts on the Mini Proto so a small metal ball bridges them when the device tilts. Firmware reads the GPIO and registers tilt events.

projects.title: DIY Water Conductivity Probe
projects.tagline: Graphite pencil leads in a glass of water give you a basic TDS meter.
projects.howItWorks: Two graphite rods become the probe on the Mini Proto. Applying a known AC-style square wave and reading the return current gives a rough conductivity estimate. Correlate with ENV III temperature for compensation.
extensions: Track pool water quality over a season

projects.title: Sunrise Alarm Clock
projects.tagline: RGB strip brightens gradually over 30 minutes — wake up without the jarring beep.
projects.howItWorks: At 30 min before the target wake time, the strip starts at deep red at 1% brightness and slowly ramps to full warm-white. A gentle chime at the target time. Keyboard or button snooze.

projects.title: Gaming Desk Reactive Lights
projects.tagline: Strip cycles through palettes by time of day and room temp — vibe for every session.
projects.howItWorks: The palette shifts: cool blues when it's hot, warm oranges when it's cool, rainbow party mode when temp is just right and it's evening. ENV III data drives the mood.

projects.title: Aquarium Smart Module
projects.tagline: Temp + light + pump control on one tidy board — and an alarm if anything drifts.
projects.howItWorks: The Mini Hub fans out to the trio of ENV III (air temp), Light (daylight), and Relay (pump). A dawn-dusk schedule drives the pump; temperature out-of-range pulses a warning.
extensions: Add a DIY water-temp probe via Mini Proto

projects.title: Portable Field Science Kit
projects.tagline: Temp + light + distance in one shoulder-bag-ready pack for field trips.
projects.howItWorks: Mini Hub lets one device carry ENV III, Light, and Ultrasonic simultaneously. A menu lets students mark a location, note observations, and save samples to flash for later review.

projects.title: Workbench Status Column
projects.tagline: Angle, joystick, and RGB on one rack — the ultimate workbench companion.
projects.howItWorks: The angle sets PWM brightness / fan speed / volume of whatever's currently selected. Joystick picks the target. RGB strip colour-codes what's active. Mini Hub carries everything.

projects.title: Garden Sensor Pod
projects.tagline: One weatherproof pod, four senses, one glanceable dashboard.
projects.howItWorks: The Mini Hub hosts ENV III, Light, and PIR. Inside a weatherproof case with a clear face, the pod tracks microclimate and animal visits throughout the day.

projects.title: Multi-Input Jukebox
projects.tagline: Scan a card, twist the knob, flick the stick — the coolest retro music controller.
projects.howItWorks: NFC picks an album; Angle scrubs through the track; joystick navigates up/down the playlist and Z-press pauses. All three plug into the Mini Hub. Commands go to a media server over Wi-Fi.

projects.title: Hallway Controller
projects.tagline: Motion detection, light-strip automation, and a relay for the ceiling lamp — one box.
projects.howItWorks: PIR triggers a warm-dim RGB glow during late-night hours, or the full ceiling lamp via relay during the day. Mini Hub keeps it tidy. 'Away' mode randomises the lights to simulate presence.

projects.title: Tiny Smart-Home Hub
projects.tagline: PIR + light + climate in one fist-sized pod that reports to Home Assistant.
projects.howItWorks: All three sensors live on one Mini Hub. Readings are posted over MQTT to Home Assistant every 60 s, and motion/light/temperature events are pushed immediately.

projects.title: Puppet Show Control Box
projects.tagline: A kid-run mini stage: servo curtains, RGB spotlight, knob for drama.
projects.howItWorks: Angle knob sets spotlight brightness. Device button triggers a servo-driven curtain open/close. RGB strip provides the backstage glow. Mini Hub ties it all together.
extensions: Add a speaker for sound cues
Second hub for a bigger stage

projects.title: LoRa Distress Beacon
projects.tagline: Press-hold the key — GPS coordinates blast out over LoRa, no phone needed.
projects.howItWorks: Long-press a labelled key; GNSS fix is acquired, and an SOS packet (GPS, name, timestamp) is repeated every 60 s at max power until cancelled. A paired base station picks it up and alerts.
extensions: Vibrate on ACK from base station

projects.title: Off-Grid Group Chat
projects.tagline: Five Cardputers, one room — you're all chatting over radio with zero Wi-Fi.
projects.howItWorks: Each device has a nickname and a shared channel. Outgoing messages are framed with sender + hash. All devices display the incoming stream chronologically; simple rate-limiting avoids flooding.

projects.title: LoRa Race Timer Station
projects.tagline: Cyclists tap start at base, finish at another — LoRa syncs the timing in real time.
projects.howItWorks: Each station broadcasts its local clock on a schedule. When a rider triggers a station (via joystick press or an external beam), the event is sent with an ID. A 'referee' device collects all events and computes splits.
extensions: GPS stamping for where they passed

projects.title: Rural Farm Sensor Network
projects.tagline: Solar nodes in the fields report temperature, humidity, and soil status to the barn.
projects.howItWorks: Each field-node sleeps most of the time; wakes every 10 min, reads ENV III and a soil probe, transmits, sleeps again. A 'base' Cardputer aggregates and charts them. Mini Hub makes each node modular.
extensions: SMS bridge from base for remote alerts

projects.title: LoRa Geocache Hunt
projects.tagline: GPS-guided treasure hunt — hot / cold clues over LoRa, no cell signal needed.
projects.howItWorks: Hosts pre-programme GPS coordinates and clues. Players follow distance indicators on the LCD; LoRa broadcasts 'hint packets' from the host when a player is stuck. Optionally flash the LED on approach.
extensions: Web leaderboard via a Wi-Fi bridge

projects.title: Air Quality Mesh
projects.tagline: A neighbourhood of Cardputers swaps air and climate readings over LoRa.
projects.howItWorks: Each node samples ENV III regularly, broadcasts a compact packet (station ID, temp, humidity, pressure). Every node accumulates a rolling table of all known stations; the Cardputer LCD shows the mesh in real time.
extensions: Add a real AQI sensor via Mini Proto
Publish to a public dashboard via a gateway

projects.title: LoRa Drone Telemetry Receiver
projects.tagline: Catch altitude + GPS from your model plane or drone at 5 km — the hobbyist's dream.
projects.howItWorks: The drone carries an airborne LoRa transmitter that emits altitude, speed, heading, and GPS. The Cardputer ground-station receives and visualises the track on a scrolling map / chart.

projects.title: MIDI Drum Pad
projects.tagline: Four felt-padded keys on the Mini Proto, fanned out through the I/O Hub — USB MIDI drums.
projects.howItWorks: Piezo sensors on the Mini Proto detect hits; the I/O Hub hosts multiple pads. Firmware debounces peaks and sends USB MIDI note-on/off with velocity derived from peak height.
extensions: Kick-drum trigger via a foot switch

projects.title: Workout Rep Counter
projects.tagline: Ultrasonic watches your barbell path — counts reps, adjusts with a sensitivity knob.
projects.howItWorks: An ultrasonic aims at the moving part of a pull-up bar, bench, or squat rack. Each sustained cycle through a range-gate counts as one rep. The angle knob adjusts sensitivity live. I/O Hub makes the wiring tidy.
